concept.headline: Rust has three loop forms: loop (infinite), while (condition-checked), and for (range/iterator).
concept.explanation: loop runs until an explicit break. while re-evaluates its condition before each iteration. for is the most common — it iterates over a range or any iterator. Unlike other languages, break can return a value from a loop expression, and there is no traditional C-style for(;;) — use ranges instead.
keyPoints: loop { } is truly infinite — must use break to exit
break value; exits loop and makes that value the result of the loop expression
while condition { } — condition re-evaluated each iteration
for x in range — range 0..5 is exclusive end; 0..=5 is inclusive
continue skips the rest of the current iteration body

demo.intro: Watch a for loop count down, then a loop expression that returns a value.

explanation.why: The inclusive range 1..=3 produces an iterator yielding 1, 2, 3
explanation.change: loop begins, i will take values 1, 2, 3
explanation.intent: Iterate a known number of times using a range
explanation.misconception: 1..=3 includes 3; 1..3 would stop at 2 — the = sign makes the end inclusive

explanation.why: The iterator yields 1 as the first element and binds it to i
explanation.intent: Process the first value of the range
explanation.misconception: i is re-bound each iteration — it is not mutated; a fresh binding is created

explanation.why: Body runs with current i value
explanation.misconception: The body runs once per yielded value, not based on a manual counter
Rust's for loop calls .next() on the iterator automatically — you never see the StopIteration signal
i inside the loop body is always the current iteration's binding

explanation.why: The iterator yields 3 (the last value, since the range is inclusive)
explanation.misconception: Because the range is 1..=3, 3 IS included — the loop runs 3 times total
After this, the iterator is exhausted — the for loop exits naturally without a break

explanation.why: The range iterator is exhausted — no more values to yield
explanation.change: loop scope closes; i is no longer accessible
explanation.intent: Automatic exit when the range is fully consumed
explanation.misconception: You do not need break to end a for loop — it ends when the iterator is done

explanation.why: break 99 exits the loop and makes 99 the value of the entire loop expression
explanation.intent: Demonstrate that loop is an expression that can produce a value
explanation.misconception: This is unique to loop (not while or for) — only loop can break with a value

explanation.why: answer holds 99, the value returned by the loop expression
explanation.intent: Verify the loop expression value was captured correctly
explanation.misconception: Without let answer = loop { ... }, the return value from break would be discarded

prediction.explanation: 0..4 is an exclusive range — it yields 0, 1, 2, 3 (four values). The loop body runs 4 times, printing 0, 1, 2, 3.

explanation.afterDemo: The for loop iterated over the inclusive range 1..=3, binding i to each value in turn. After the range was exhausted, the loop exited automatically. Then a loop expression used break 99 to return a value — showing that loop, like if, is an expression in Rust.
keyTakeaways: for x in range is the idiomatic way to loop a known number of times.
0..n is exclusive end (n not included); 0..=n is inclusive.
loop { break value; } is an expression that returns a value.

practice.prompt: Use a for loop to print each number from 1 to 5 (inclusive), each on its own line.
practice.hint: Use 1..=5 for an inclusive range. println!("{}", i) inside the body.

feedback.successMessage: Correct! 1..=5 iterates 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 — each printed on its own line.
retryHints: Use 1..=5 (with =) to include 5. Using 1..5 would stop at 4.
Each println!("{}", i) call outputs one line.
Expected output: five lines, each containing just the number (1 through 5).

summary.title: Loops — Complete
takeaways: for x in range is the idiomatic counted loop — no manual counter needed.
loop runs until break; it can return a value with break value.
while condition checks its guard before each iteration.
